name: Germany
summary: Severe software/AI talent shortage and tuition-free public MSc make Germany a strong study->work->PR route; dual citizenship allowed but B1 German, high tax and rising anti-immigrant politics temper it.

links.title: Make it in Germany (official portal)

job-market.summary: Acute software/AI shortage; ~109k unfilled IT roles, strong demand for both engineers.
job-market.reasoning: Bitkom 2025 reports a shortage of ~109,000 IT specialists and 137,000+ open software roles, with an average 7.7 months to fill; demand easily absorbs two CS graduates and EU Blue Card thresholds are routinely met by IT salaries.
evidence: Bitkom 2025: ~109,000 IT specialists missing; 137,000+ open roles
Average IT vacancy takes 7.7 months to fill
IT named a shortage occupation with lower Blue Card threshold (€45,934 in 2026)
Berlin/Munich/Hamburg tech scenes hire in English

links.title: EU Blue Card Germany (Make it in Germany, official)
Skilled professionals for Germany (BMWE, official)

visa-access.summary: Decision and biometrics in Dhaka, but APS, ~€11,904 blocked account and 12-month wait are heavy hurdles.
visa-access.reasoning: The German Embassy Dhaka decides and takes biometrics locally (no New Delhi travel), but Bangladeshi students face a mandatory APS certificate, a blocked account of €11,904 (€992/month for 2026/27), and an average reported wait of 12+ months for a student-visa appointment.
evidence: German Mission Dhaka decides national/student visas locally
APS certificate mandatory for all Bangladeshi applicants since 2022
Blocked account €11,904/year (€992/month) for 2026/27
Reported average student-visa wait over 12 months

links.title: Study Visa information (German Embassy Dhaka, official)
Student Visa FAQ (German Embassy Dhaka, official)

citizenship.summary: Standard 5 years + dual citizenship allowed, but 3-year fast-track repealed Oct 2025 and B1 + test required.
citizenship.reasoning: The 2024 reform keeps the 5-year naturalisation path and permits dual citizenship for Bangladeshis, but the 3-year 'turbo' route was repealed effective 30 Oct 2025; applicants still need B1 German, the Leben-in-Deutschland test and financial self-sufficiency.
evidence: Standard naturalisation reduced from 8 to 5 years (StAG 2024)
Dual citizenship retained for non-EU nationals
3-year fast-track repealed, in force from 30 Oct 2025
B1 German + 33-question integration test + no reliance on benefits

links.title: Naturalisation in Germany (BAMF, official)
German nationality law (BMI, official)

post-study-work.summary: 18-month post-study job-search permit, unrestricted work, in-country switch to work visa.
post-study-work.reasoning: German-university graduates get an 18-month residence permit to seek qualified work with unrestricted employment during it, then switch to a work permit or EU Blue Card from inside Germany; spouse can work in parallel.
evidence: 18-month post-study job-search permit for German graduates
Unrestricted work allowed during the search
Switch to work permit / Blue Card without leaving Germany
Proof of ~€992/month funds during search

links.title: Jobs after studies (Make it in Germany, official)
Opportunity Card / Chancenkarte (Make it in Germany, official)

spouse-family.summary: Both can hold own permits; spouse of skilled worker/Blue Card gets immediate unrestricted work.
spouse-family.reasoning: As two qualified engineers each can hold an independent study or work permit, and a reuniting spouse of a skilled worker or EU Blue Card holder gets immediate unrestricted labour-market access with the A1 language requirement waived for Blue Card families.
evidence: Spouse of skilled worker/Blue Card holder may work without restriction
A1 German waived for spouses joining Blue Card holders
Both partners can instead each hold their own student/work permit
Sponsor income ~€1,200/month for two persons

links.title: Family reunification (Make it in Germany, official)

msc-study.summary: Abundant English MSc, no tuition at most public universities, DAAD scholarships, student work rights.
msc-study.reasoning: Most public universities charge only a ~€150–350 semester fee with broad English-taught CS Master's catalogues and DAAD funding; students may work 120 full / 240 half days a year and the spouse can accompany. Exceptions: Baden-Württemberg (€1,500/sem) and TUM (€4,000–6,000/sem).
evidence: No tuition at most public universities; semester fee only
Large English-taught MSc CS catalogue via DAAD
Student work allowance 120 full / 240 half days per year
Exceptions: Baden-Württemberg €1,500/sem, TUM €4,000–6,000/sem

links.title: DAAD International Programmes database (official)
DAAD scholarships (official)

pr-pathway.summary: Settlement permit in 21 months (B1) or 27 (A1) on Blue Card; study time can count.
pr-pathway.reasoning: EU Blue Card holders qualify for a settlement permit after 21 months with B1 German or 27 with A1, far faster than the standard 5 years; for German-university graduates, study time can count toward the residence requirement.
evidence: Settlement permit at 21 months with B1 on Blue Card
27 months with A1 German on Blue Card
Standard route 5 years; study time may count for graduates
Requires pension contributions, B1 and integration test

links.title: Settlement permit (Make it in Germany, official)
The EU Blue Card (BAMF, official)

income-cost.summary: Good gross SW pay but 30-40% deductions and tight metro rents; two incomes help.
income-cost.reasoning: Mid-level engineers earn ~€55,000–75,000 gross but take-home is only ~60–70% after tax and social contributions, and Munich/Berlin rents are high; two engineering salaries still allow meaningful savings with budgeting (~15–25% of net in Berlin).
evidence: Mid-level SW gross ~€55k–75k; Berlin avg ~€73k
Deductions typically 30–40% of gross
One-bed city-centre rent ~€1,000–1,400 (Berlin), €1,200–1,800 (Munich)
Two incomes enable ~15–25% net savings with budgeting

links.title: Salary, taxes & social security (Make it in Germany, official)

healthcare.summary: Universal statutory health insurance; affordable student cover and strong welfare.
healthcare.reasoning: Mandatory statutory health insurance (GKV) covers residents comprehensively, students get a discounted public rate, and welfare benefits (e.g. Elterngeld) are strong; quality is high for both partners.
evidence: Statutory health insurance (GKV) universal for residents
Discounted public student insurance rate
Comprehensive coverage and parental allowance (Elterngeld)

links.title: Health insurance (Make it in Germany, official)

culture-language.summary: English works in tech, but B1 German is mandatory for citizenship and daily officialdom.
culture-language.reasoning: Tech workplaces frequently operate in English, but German is required for bureaucracy, faster PR (B1) and naturalisation (B1 + integration test), and daily life outside major hubs, a real barrier to their citizenship goal.
evidence: English common in tech workplaces
B1 German required for PR and citizenship
Integration test (Leben in Deutschland) for naturalisation
German needed for officialdom and daily life

links.title: Learning German (Make it in Germany, official)

safety-law.summary: Top-tier rule of law and order/security; strong legal protections.
safety-law.reasoning: Germany ranks in the top tier of the WJP Rule of Law Index 2025 (top-5 for order and security) with low violent crime and established anti-discrimination law, though immigrants face rising societal hostility.
evidence: WJP Rule of Law Index 2025: top tier, top-5 order & security
Low violent crime relative to peers
Established anti-discrimination framework

links.title: WJP Rule of Law Index 2025 – Germany

politics.summary: Stable institutions but CDU-led tightening, AfD ~21% second, family-reunion curbs.
politics.reasoning: Institutions are stable under the CDU/SPD coalition and skilled-worker pathways are preserved, but migration policy is tightening (fast-track citizenship repealed, family reunification curbed) and the AfD's ~21% second-place finish signals a rising anti-immigrant trend.
evidence: CDU/SPD coalition under Merz; skilled-worker routes kept
AfD ~21%, second place in 2025 federal election
Family reunification for subsidiary protection suspended; tougher migration line
3-year fast-track citizenship abolished

links.title: German elections 2025 and immigration (Fragomen)

tax.summary: High burden: progressive income tax plus ~20% social contributions.
tax.reasoning: Income tax rises to 42% (45% above ~€277,825) and roughly 20% in employee social contributions push total deductions to about 30–40% of gross, leaving higher earners with only ~55–60% take-home.
evidence: Top marginal income-tax rate 42% (45% above ~€277,825)
Employee social contributions ~20% of gross
Total deductions typically 30–40% of gross
Basic allowance €12,096 (2025)

links.title: Salary, taxes & social security (Make it in Germany, official)

muslim-diaspora.summary: ~5.5M Muslims, 2,500+ mosques, halal ubiquitous, but small Bangladeshi community and sharply rising Islamophobia.
muslim-diaspora.reasoning: Germany has ~5.5 million Muslims and 2,500+ mosques with halal food and worship easy in cities, but the Bangladeshi community is small (South Asian presence is mainly Pakistani), and anti-Muslim incidents rose ~60% in 2024 amid AfD's rise.
evidence: ~5.5 million Muslims (~6.6%); 2,500+ mosques
Halal food widely available in major cities
Bangladeshi community small; South Asian presence mostly Pakistani (~140k)
CLAIM 2025: ~60% rise in anti-Muslim incidents in 2024 (3,000+ cases)

links.title: Islam in Germany (overview)
EU FRA anti-Muslim hatred database
